top.title: 'What if we're left with ruins?': Doubts creep in for Iranians who supported war
top.summary: Teachers, engineers and shopkeepers tell the BBC they fear Iran falling into chaos.

top.title: Rapper-politician Balendra Shah's party wins Nepal election
top.summary: The Rastriya Swatatantra Party has won a large majority in an election dominated by issues including corruption.

top.title: China approves 'ethnic unity' law requiring minorities to learn Mandarin
top.summary: The law states that children should be taught Mandarin before kindergarten, until the end of high school.

top.title: Grammarly pulls AI author-impersonation tool after backlash
top.summary: Writers criticised the feature which used their names and styles as "AI personas" without consent.

top.title: Lloyds, Bank of Scotland and Halifax apps showed customers other users' transactions
top.summary: The Lloyds Banking Group customers reported being able to view payments and charges from other sources.

top.title: What role has cyber warfare played in Iran?
top.summary: Militaries are often cagey about their cyber activities. But the US has hinted at the role it has played.
